items.headline: Former Iowa State star Audi Crooks commits to Oklahoma State
items.description: Former Iowa State center Audi Crooks, No. 1 in ESPN's women's basketball transfer rankings, has committed to Oklahoma State, she announced Sunday night.

items.headline: Green scores 36 points, Suns lock down Warriors, Curry in 111-96 win in NBA's play-in tourney
items.description: — Jalen Green scored 36 points, Devin Booker added 20 and the Phoenix Suns locked down Stephen Curry and the Golden State Warriors, winning 111-96 in the NBA's play-in tournament Friday night.

items.headline: Magic rout Hornets 121-90 in play-in game, advance to face Pistons in 1st round of playoffs
items.description: — Paolo Banchero scored 25 points and the Orlando Magic built a 35-point lead in the first half on the way to a 121-90 rout of Charlotte Hornets in a play-in tournament game on Friday night and advanced to a first-round playoff matchup against the...

items.headline: Women's NCAA basketball transfer rankings for 2026-27 season
items.description: Audi Crooks is headed to Oklahoma St., Jordan Lee is bound for South Carolina and Kymora Johnson is staying at U.Va.

items.headline: 36ers captain's contract release after NBL finals loss
items.description: Dejan Vasiljevic won't return for the final year of his contract with Adelaide after the 36ers signalled their intent to move "in a different direction" from the co-captain.

items.headline: Rinku banishes his boggart to finally win KKR a game
items.description: It might not have been so had Nandre Burger not shelled a chance, had Ravindra Jadeja bowled his fourth over, but Rinku Singh played what was in front of him to end KKR's losing streak

items.headline: Haas out of Origin as fellow Broncos prop hospitalised
items.description: Brisbane's injury woes have gone beyond crisis point, with prop Corey Jensen in hospital with a blood clot in his lung.

items.headline: Wembanyama scores 35 points in playoff debut as Spurs roll past Trail Blazers 111-98
items.description: — Victor Wembanyama scored 35 points in his playoff debut and the San Antonio Spurs rolled to a 111-98 victory over the Portland Trail Blazers in Game 1 of their first-round Western Conference series Sunday night.
